title: CircuitBreaker has redundant manual Default impl and split impl blocks
description: delivery.rs:27-38 and 34-38: CircuitBreaker has a new() that just creates HashMap::new(), then a separate manual Default impl that calls new(). #[derive(Default)] produces identical behavior. The two separate impl blocks (lines 27 and 40) should also be merged into one. Clippy lint: new_without_default already catches the inverse of this pattern.
close_reason: CircuitBreaker now uses #[derive(Default)], split impl blocks merged
